text: T cells hunt infected cells and coordinate immune responses. But how do they do it? T cells are a type of white blood cell crucial for your immune defense. They recognize and destroy cells that are infected. Imagine tiny warriors navigating your body, seeking out threats. This is how T cells patrol your system. Recent studies show T cells also help other immune cells function better, like a conductor in an orchestra. So, without T cells, your body would struggle to fend off infections. They are vital to your health! Curious about more immune secrets? Hit like and follow for more science insights!
